title: Baalbeck
caption: The band at architectural scale: ruin, crowd, myth, and political consequence in one image.

title: Not a Band
caption: Mashrou' Leila was a public system disguised as a band.
body: A temporary public sphere built from songs, bodies, images, press, risk, and collective recognition.

title: AUB / Origin
caption: The project began as a collective inside the architecture and social life of AUB.

title: Self Titled EP
caption: The origin surface before the project became institution mythology and pressure system.

title: Rehearsal Room
caption: Collective authorship began in ordinary rooms where sound bodies and equipment had to negotiate one another.

title: Counter-Public Infrastructure
caption: The visible band sat on top of a larger operating architecture.
body: Sound, stage, image, community, crisis response, press narrative, and audience safety had to move as one system.

title: Plan de Beyrouth
caption: An early graphic plan turned Beirut into a layered stage of memory language color and public life.

title: El Hal Romancy
caption: The breakthrough album moved the project from campus mythology into a regional touring organism.

title: Raasuk
caption: A direct-to-fan record and early crowdfunding proof point for an Arabic independent audience.

title: Scale
caption: Specificity travelled further than the market expected.
body: 500M+ cross-platform streams|1.1M monthly listeners at peak|50+ countries toured|35,000-40,000 capacity venue moments|$60K+ direct-to-fan crowdfunding

title: The Design Problem
caption: How do you make a fragile public visible without making it easy to destroy?
body: Every poster, video, interview, stage image, and show announcement had to carry desire, ambiguity, protection, and refusal at the same time.

title: Collective Portrait
caption: The public image kept individuality visible inside a shared authored world.

title: Ibn El Leil
caption: The nocturnal visual universe made the band legible as a complete world.

title: Production Room
caption: Recording and post-production translated collective performance into a durable sonic object.

title: Roman
caption: The music video became a visual argument about gender, power, and the politics of representation.

title: Tiny Desk Concert
caption: The band compressed its live force into a bare room and still carried the full architecture of the project.

title: L'Olympia
caption: The project travelled as a complete stage language: light body audience and mythology.

title: Rolling Stone
caption: The cover formalized a regional counter-public as a media fact.

title: Crisis Operations
caption: The stage offered visibility but not protection.
body: Censorship and bans: Jordan (2016, 2017) | Egypt (Cairo concert crackdown, 2017) | Lebanon (Byblos cancellation, 2019) | Real-time security, audience safety, and legal defense managed independently.

title: Stage Installation
caption: Scenography was developed as spatial infrastructure before it reached the scale of a live venue.

title: Stage Architecture
caption: The live system had to turn sound into a room the audience could enter.

title: The Band As System
caption: A band that became cultural infrastructure: sound, image, stage, audience, and risk moving as one system.
